methodology.scorer: Regex-based with strict validation (names only counted with accompanying data values)
methodology.statistical_rigor: N=5 runs minimum for key findings, 95% CIs, paired t-tests

finding_1_basic_vs_advanced.claim: Basic attacks create false sense of security; advanced attacks reveal true vulnerability

finding_2_size_vs_vulnerability.claim: For OpenAI models: smaller = more vulnerable. CIs non-overlapping.

finding_3_claude_resistance.claim: Claude architecture more resistant than OpenAI. Haiku and Sonnet differ due to different training, not size.

data.note: Different model generations — not a size comparison

finding_4_defense_effectiveness.claim: Defense-in-depth achieves 0% ASR against advanced attacks across all models

data.note: Previous 6.2% was scorer false positive — corrected to 0%

finding_5_ablation.claim: Context Separation alone achieves similar ASR as all 6 defenses combined

finding_6_crosslingual_static.claim: Portuguese and English static attacks have similar baseline ASR. Defenses block Portuguese more aggressively.

finding_7_crescendo.claim: Crescendo achieves 100% success on baseline. Portuguese produces 23% more evidence.

finding_8_adaptive_vs_defended.claim: Adaptive attacks (PAIR, 15 iterations) fail against defended agent

finding_9_utility.claim: Defended agent maintains 73.3% utility on legitimate queries

per_category_breakdown.note: Advanced attacks 100% effective on all OpenAI, 33% on Claude/Phi3
per_category_breakdown.side_channel: Only category effective against commercial models with basic attacks (40%)

internal: System prompt grants full access — model following instructions is correct behavior
Advanced attacks target known GPT weaknesses — potential bias toward finding GPT vulnerable
external: Only 3 tools (DB, file read, file write) — real agents have more
In-memory SQLite — real deployments use persistent databases
construct: Scorer validated on 10 cases — larger validation set would be stronger
LLM judge disagreed with regex scorer 82% of the time (pre-fix) — methodology sensitive to scoring approach
